title: Exception: Not found column Timestamp in block during merge on GraphiteMergeTree/ReplicatedGraphiteMergeTree  Table
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
After upgrading from v25.3 to v25.8 MERGE_PARTS operations started to fail. This seems to only fail for "old" data where the Timestamp column's TTL value  has been exceeded(see schema in the how to reproduce section)
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
v25.7, v25.8, v25.10
I haven't tried v25.9
v25.6 is unaffected
### How to reproduce
Use clickhouse v25.8
1. I used the schema below
```sql
CREATE DATABASE graphite;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graphite.graphite_tmp (
Path String CODEC(ZSTD(3)),
Value Float64 CODEC(Gorilla, LZ4),
Time UInt32 CODEC(DoubleDelta, LZ4),
Date Date CODEC(DoubleDelta, LZ4),
Timestamp UInt32 CODEC(DoubleDelta, LZ4) TTL Date + INTERVAL 1 DAY
) ENGINE = GraphiteMergeTree('graphite_rollup')
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(Date)
ORDER BY (Path, Time)
TTL Date + INTERVAL 1 YEAR;
```
For configuration I am using defaults. Happy to dump it out if necessary.
2. I then inserted data in the 202503 partition (I had data from another table here)
```sql
INSERT INTO graphite.graphite_tmp (Date, Time, Path, Value, Timestamp)
SELECT
toDate('2025-03-01') AS Date,
toDateTime('2025-03-01 00:00:00') + number * 30 AS Time,
concat('servers.', toString(intDiv(number, 1000)), '.', arrayElement(['cpu.load','mem.used','disk.io'], (number % 3) + 1)) AS Path,
50 + 10 * sin(number / 200) + rand() % 5 AS Value,
1 AS Version
FROM numbers(1000000000);
```
3. Watch the log once the MERGE_PARTS operations starts you'll see the stacktrace.
### Expected behavior
On versions before v25.7 the merge would succeed without any issue.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Below are stack traces I got on both ReplicatedGraphiteMergeTree and GraphiteMergeTree
**ReplicatedGraphiteMergeTree**
```
Code: 10. DB::Exception: Not found column Timestamp in block. There are only columns: Path, Time, Value, Date. (NOT_FOUND_COLUMN_IN_BLOCK), Stack trace (when copying this message, always include the lines below):
0. DB::Exception::Exception(DB::Exception::MessageMasked&&, int, bool) @ 0x00000000133ab85f
1. DB::Exception::Exception(String&&, int, String, bool) @ 0x000000000c8569ce
2. DB::Exception::Exception(PreformattedMessage&&, int) @ 0x000000000c856480
3. DB::Exception::Exception<String const&, String>(int, FormatStringHelperImpl<std::type_identity<String const&>::type, std::type_identity<String>::type>, String const&, String&&) @ 0x000000000d85812b
4. DB::Block::getPositionByName(String const&, bool) const @ 0x0000000016a8ab24
5. DB::GraphiteRollupSortedAlgorithm::GraphiteRollupSortedAlgorithm(std::shared_ptr<DB::Block const>, unsigned long, DB::SortDescription, unsigned long, unsigned long, DB::Graphite::Params, long) @ 0x0000000019edf512
bug_features_seed: CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS test_db;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_db.test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, value DOUBLE PRECISION, timestamp INT) PRIMARY KEY (id);
INSERT INTO test_db.test_table (id, name, value, timestamp) VALUES (1, 'test1', 10.5, 1), (2, 'test2', 20.0, 2), (3, 'test3', 30.0, 3);
INSERT INTO test_db.test_table (id, name, value, timestamp) SELECT number, CONCAT('test', number), number * 1.5, number FROM generate_series(1, 5) AS number;
SELECT * FROM test_db.test_table WHERE timestamp IS NULL OR timestamp > 2;
key_bug_features: MERGE_PARTS operations fail for old data with exceeded TTL
